author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
title: The Seventh Breviate.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: “The next question we shall have to propose,” he says, “is, whether God wishes that man be without sin.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Beyond doubt God wishes it; and no doubt he has the ability.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: For who is so foolhardy as to hesitate to believe that to be possible, which he has no doubt about God’s wishing?”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: If God wished not that man should be without sin, He would not have sent His Son without sin, to heal men of their sins.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: This takes place in believers who are being renewed day by day, until their righteousness becomes perfect, like fully restored health.
